title: Measure what matters: the four numbers of a leasing business
dek: You cannot fix a business you refuse to look at. Four numbers, tracked weekly, tell you everything.
heroAlt: Measure what matters: the four numbers of a leasing business

blocks.html: Most agents track one number: the commission that just hit. It is the most emotionally satisfying metric and the least useful, because it describes decisions made two months ago. By the time revenue dips, the actual problem is ancient history.

A business runs on leading indicators, the numbers upstream of the money. In leasing there are four, and they take five minutes a week to record: new conversations, tours booked, applications submitted, leases signed. That is the entire funnel, and every income problem you will ever have lives at exactly one of those stages.

blocks.text: Revenue is a lagging rumor. The funnel is the live feed.

items.title: Conversations low?
items.html: A prospecting problem. Nothing downstream can compensate. Fix the morning hour first, always.

items.title: Tours low, conversations fine?
items.html: A qualifying or offer problem. You are talking to the wrong people or giving them no reason to meet.

items.title: Applications low, tours fine?
items.html: A closing problem. Advisor skills, objection handling, the ask. This is a skills gap, and skills are trainable.

items.title: Signatures low, applications fine?
items.html: A process problem. Paperwork speed, building relationships, follow-through. Usually the fastest fix on the board.

blocks.html: Open a note and log last week's four numbers from memory. If you cannot, that is the finding. Start the tally Monday; the patterns show up by week three.
Building A Rental Business, Course 8, turns the four numbers into a full financial and operations dashboard you review in minutes.

blocks.lead: Course 8 builds the full operating dashboard.
